proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher age_years is associated with longer pfs_months (positive correlation).
Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months (positive correlation).
Higher ki67_pct is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).
Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).
Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher crp_mg_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher nlr (neutrophil-lymphocyte ratio) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Larger tumor_size_cm is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Strong positive correlation: r=+0.7015, p<1e-300. age_years explains ~49% of variance in PFS univariately.
Positive correlation: r=+0.1002, p=9.4e-112.
Negative correlation: r=-0.0914, p=3.0e-93.
Negative correlation: r=-0.1155, p=4.1e-148.
Weak negative correlation: r=-0.0164, p=2.5e-4.
No association: r=-0.0033, p=0.46.
No association: r=+0.0028, p=0.54.

proposed_hypotheses.text: stage_iv=1 patients have shorter pfs_months than stage_iv=0 patients.
has_brain_mets=1 patients have shorter pfs_months than has_brain_mets=0 patients.
Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months (monotonic).
er_positive=1 patients have longer pfs_months than er_positive=0 patients.
pr_positive=1 patients have longer pfs_months than pr_positive=0 patients.
her2_positive=1 patients have shorter pfs_months than her2_positive=0 patients.
pik3ca_mutation=1 patients have shorter pfs_months than pik3ca_mutation=0 patients.
brca1_mutation or brca2_mutation positivity is associated with shorter pfs_months.
postmenopausal status is associated with different pfs_months relative to pre-menopausal status.
node_positive=1 patients have shorter pfs_months than node_positive=0 patients.
sex_female has a main effect on pfs_months in this breast cohort.

analyses.result_summary: stage_iv=1 mean PFS 3.617 vs stage_iv=0 mean PFS 5.153 (diff -1.536 mo, p≈0).
brain_mets=1 mean PFS 3.750 vs 4.800 (diff -1.050, p=1.5e-178).
ECOG 0/1/2 mean PFS = 5.640 / 4.444 / 3.289; ANOVA F=2860.8, p≈0. Strong monotonic decrement.
pik3ca_mutation=1 mean PFS 4.328 vs 4.887 (diff -0.559, p=6.1e-144).

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind by brca1_mutation; same for brca2
analyses.result_summary: BRCA1: diff -0.024 (p=0.72). BRCA2: diff -0.127 (p=0.081). No clear univariate effect.
Postmenopausal vs pre: 4.684 vs 4.707 (diff -0.023, p=0.31). No effect.
node+=1 vs 0: 4.681 vs 4.705 (diff -0.024, p=0.29). No effect.
Female vs male: 4.686 vs 4.699 (diff -0.013, p=0.56). No detectable effect; cohort is 44.7% female (likely includes male breast cancer plus possibly mislabeled records).

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable OLS for pfs_months including all features and treatments, age_years, ecog_ps, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, weight_loss_pct_6mo, albumin_g_dl, ki67_pct, er_positive, her2_positive, pik3ca_mutation, ldh_u_l, and treatment_palbociclib will each have independent effects with the directions seen in univariate analysis.
her2_low=1 has only a marginal independent association with pfs_months once HER2-positive and other features are adjusted for.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted R^2=0.792. Independent effects (coef, p): age_years +0.176/yr (p≈0), ecog_ps -1.171/level (p≈0), stage_iv -1.549 (p≈0), has_brain_mets -0.983 (p≈0), weight_loss_pct_6mo -0.077/% (p≈0), albumin_g_dl +0.476/(g/dL) (p≈0), ki67_pct -0.019/% (p≈0), er_positive +0.532 (p=8.6e-275), her2_positive -0.471 (p=2.4e-239), pik3ca_mutation -0.582 (p≈0), ldh_u_l -5.2e-4/(U/L) (p=2.2e-16). All confirm the univariate directions and survive adjustment.

analyses.code: OLS coefficient on her2_low in full model
analyses.result_summary: her2_low coefficient +0.0019, p=0.86. No independent association with PFS once HER2-positive and the other covariates are adjusted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_tamoxifen=1 patients have longer pfs_months than treatment_tamoxifen=0 patients.
treatment_palbociclib=1 patients have longer pfs_months than treatment_palbociclib=0 patients.
treatment_trastuzumab=1 patients have longer pfs_months than treatment_trastuzumab=0 patients.
treatment_olaparib=1 patients have longer pfs_months than treatment_olaparib=0 patients.
treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan=1 patients have longer pfs_months than treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan=0 patients.
treatment_pembrolizumab=1 patients have longer pfs_months than treatment_pembrolizumab=0 patients.

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS 4.723 (on) vs 4.680 (off), diff +0.043 mo, p=0.077.
Mean PFS 5.408 (on) vs 4.310 (off), diff +1.098 mo, p≈0. Strong positive main effect.
Mean PFS 4.645 (on) vs 4.705 (off), diff -0.060 mo, p=0.029. Significant but trivially small and negative — no average benefit.
Mean PFS 4.660 (on) vs 4.696 (off), diff -0.036, p=0.33. Null.
Mean PFS 4.686 (on) vs 4.694 (off), diff -0.007, p=0.85. Null.
Mean PFS 4.630 (on) vs 4.704 (off), diff -0.074, p=0.018. Significant in the negative direction — slight harm or confounding.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment_palbociclib effect on pfs_months is concentrated in er_positive=1 patients (positive interaction palbociclib×er_positive).
The treatment_palbociclib effect on pfs_months is abolished in her2_positive=1 patients (negative interaction palbociclib×her2_positive).

analyses.code: OLS pfs ~ palb + er + palb*er, plus stratified means
analyses.result_summary: Stratified: ER+ palb effect +1.577 mo (5.890 vs 4.313, p≈0); ER- palb effect -0.017 mo (p=0.63). OLS interaction palbociclib×er_positive coef=+1.559 (p≈0 unadjusted; p≈0 adjusted).

analyses.code: OLS pfs ~ palb + her2_positive + palb*her2_positive, plus stratified means
analyses.result_summary: HER2- palb effect +1.350 mo (p≈0); HER2+ palb effect -0.014 mo (p=0.76). Interaction coef = -1.338 (p≈0 adjusted).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment_palbociclib effect on pfs_months is abolished by pik3ca_mutation=1 (negative interaction palbociclib×pik3ca_mutation).
The treatment_palbociclib effect on pfs_months is larger in pr_positive=1 than in pr_positive=0 patients.

analyses.result_summary: PIK3CA-WT palb effect +1.659 (p≈0); PIK3CA-mut palb effect +0.036 (p=0.87). Interaction coef=-1.665 (p≈0). PIK3CA mutation completely abolishes the palbociclib benefit.
PR- palb effect +0.399; PR+ palb effect +1.491. Interaction coef +1.030 (p≈0). PR-positivity strengthens the palbociclib effect (likely partly through correlation with ER status).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the ER+/HER2- subgroup, treatment_palbociclib has a much larger PFS benefit (>1.5 mo) than in the full cohort.

analyses.result_summary: In ER+/HER2- (n=28,589): palbociclib on=6.236 vs off=4.312, diff +1.924 mo, p≈0. Effect roughly doubles vs whole-cohort estimate.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-WT, treatment_palbociclib increases pfs_months by ~3 months on average; PIK3CA mutation negates the benefit even within ER+/HER2-.

analyses.code: sub = ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-WT vs ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-mut; t-test palbociclib in each
analyses.result_summary: ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-WT (n=18,681): palb 7.209 vs 4.300, diff +2.908 mo, p≈0. ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-mut (n=9,908): palb 4.357 vs 4.335, diff +0.022, p=0.62. PIK3CA mutation eliminates the palbociclib benefit even within the canonical ER+/HER2- subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_tamoxifen has a larger PFS benefit in er_positive=1 than in er_positive=0 patients.
treatment_tamoxifen has a larger PFS benefit in postmenopausal=1 than in pre-menopausal patients (within er_positive=1).

analyses.result_summary: ER+ tamoxifen effect +0.023 mo (p=0.45). ER- tamoxifen effect +0.081 mo (p=0.030). The ER- result is opposite to the canonical clinical expectation; both effect sizes are tiny. No meaningful tamoxifen × ER interaction.

analyses.code: stratified t-tests within ER+ by postmenopausal
analyses.result_summary: ER+/postmenopausal: +0.010 mo (p=0.81). ER+/pre-menopausal: +0.044 (p=0.38). Both null.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_trastuzumab has a positive PFS effect that is concentrated in her2_positive=1 patients.
treatment_trastuzumab has a positive PFS effect in her2_low=1 patients (extension to HER2-low biology).

analyses.result_summary: HER2+ trastuzumab effect -0.079 (p=0.15) — wrong direction and NS. HER2- effect -0.051 (p=0.10). No detectable trastuzumab benefit in HER2+ patients in this dataset.
HER2-low: -0.016 (p=0.73). HER2-/not-low: -0.080 (p=0.059). No benefit in either.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_olaparib has a positive PFS effect concentrated in patients with brca1_mutation=1 or brca2_mutation=1.

analyses.code: stratified t-test by (brca1 OR brca2)==1; adjusted OLS in BRCA+ subgroup
analyses.result_summary: BRCA1 or BRCA2 (n=2499): olaparib on=4.933 vs off=4.587, diff +0.346, p=0.041 (unadjusted). BRCA-: -0.056 (p=0.14). After adjustment for age/ecog/stage/brain_mets/er/her2 within BRCA+, olaparib coef=+0.044 (p=0.62) — the marginal positive signal vanishes after covariate adjustment, so any olaparib benefit is small and likely confounded.

analyses.code: OLS pfs ~ covariates + olaparib in BRCA1/2+ subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted olaparib effect within BRCA1/2+: +0.044 mo, p=0.62. Not robust.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan has a positive PFS effect concentrated in TNBC (er_positive=0 AND pr_positive=0 AND her2_positive=0) patients.
treatment_pembrolizumab has a positive PFS effect concentrated in TNBC patients.

analyses.code: stratified t-test by TNBC; OLS in TNBC subgroup
analyses.result_summary: TNBC (n=10,496): sacituzumab on=4.228 vs off=4.314, diff -0.085, p=0.22. Adjusted OLS coef in TNBC: +0.014 (p=0.32). No detectable benefit in TNBC.

analyses.code: stratified t-test by TNBC; OLS in TNBC subgroup
analyses.result_summary: TNBC pembrolizumab effect +0.072 (p=0.22). Non-TNBC effect -0.114 (p=0.0017) — slight harm. Adjusted OLS in TNBC: +0.011 (p=0.35). No meaningful pembrolizumab benefit anywhere.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-WT, the treatment_palbociclib PFS effect declines as ki67_pct increases (negative palbociclib×ki67_pct interaction in the adjusted model).
In the full cohort, treatment_palbociclib effect declines with rising ki67_pct (adjusted T×ki67 interaction is negative and significant).

analyses.code: OLS pfs ~ covariates + palb + ki67 + palb*ki67
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted palbociclib×ki67_pct interaction: coef=-0.0532 per ki67%, p≈0. Across the full cohort, every 1-point rise in ki67 reduces palbociclib's incremental PFS by ~0.05 mo.

analyses.code: OLS within ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-WT: pfs ~ covariates + palb + ki67 + palb*ki67
analyses.result_summary: In ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-WT: palbociclib×ki67 interaction coef=-0.138 per ki67%, p≈0. Main palb coef at ki67=0 is +5.06.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-WT, treatment_palbociclib produces large PFS gains in the lowest ki67_pct tertile, intermediate gains in the middle tertile, and no detectable gain in the highest tertile.

analyses.code: tertile split of ki67 within ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-WT, t-test palbociclib in each
analyses.result_summary: Low ki67 (<8.9): palb +4.944 mo (p≈0, n=2179 vs 3967). Mid (8.9–16.5): +3.704 mo (p≈0). High (>16.5): -0.017 mo (p=0.77). Strong dose-response — palbociclib only works in low/mid-proliferation tumors within the otherwise-eligible subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-WT, the treatment_palbociclib PFS benefit is concentrated below ki67_pct≈14, with effect ≥+4.5 months below this cutoff and essentially zero above it.

analyses.code: sweep ki67 cutoffs (10/14/15/18/20) within ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-WT, t-test palbociclib
analyses.result_summary: ki67<10: +4.96 mo (p≈0); ki67<14: +4.94 mo (p≈0, n=3829 vs 6923); ki67<15: +4.69 mo (p≈0); ki67<18: +4.10 mo (p≈0); ki67<20: +3.85 mo (p≈0). Above each cutoff: ki67≥14 gives +0.07 (p=0.18), ki67≥15 gives +0.02 (p=0.78), ki67≥18 gives -0.05 (p=0.42), ki67≥20 gives -0.03 (p=0.61). Sharp threshold near ki67=14: above it palbociclib is null.

proposed_hypotheses.text: All four predicates — er_positive=1, her2_positive=0, pik3ca_mutation=0, ki67_pct<14 — are individually binding for the maximal palbociclib effect (~+4.94 mo); dropping any one materially reduces the estimated effect.

analyses.code: compare palbociclib effect across the full subgroup vs subgroup with each predicate dropped
analyses.result_summary: Full subgroup (all 4 predicates): +4.944 mo (p≈0). Drop er_positive: +3.526. Drop her2_positive=0: +4.063. Drop pik3ca_mutation=0: +3.222. Drop ki67<14: +2.908. Each predicate adds 1–2 mo of incremental treatment benefit, confirming all four are binding modifiers.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The palbociclib PFS benefit in ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-WT/ki67<14 survives adjustment for age_years, ecog_ps, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, weight_loss_pct_6mo, albumin_g_dl, and ki67_pct.

analyses.code: OLS in ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-WT/ki67<14: pfs ~ covariates + palb
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted palbociclib coef = +4.977 mo (p≈0, n=10,752). Effect size is essentially identical to the unadjusted +4.944 mo, indicating the subgroup signal is not confounded by demographics, ECOG, stage, brain mets, weight loss, or albumin.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment within their canonical biomarker subgroups, none of trastuzumab (HER2+), olaparib (BRCA1/2+), sacituzumab (TNBC), pembrolizumab (TNBC), or tamoxifen (ER+) show a meaningful (≥0.2 mo) PFS benefit.

analyses.code: OLS adjusted treatment coef in each canonical subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Trastuzumab in HER2+: +0.006 mo (p=0.60, n=9060). Olaparib in BRCA1/2+: +0.044 mo (p=0.62, n=2499). Sacituzumab in TNBC: +0.014 mo (p=0.32, n=10,496). Pembrolizumab in TNBC: +0.011 mo (p=0.35, n=10,496). Tamoxifen in ER+: +0.008 mo (p=0.67, n=34,886). All five are flat after adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A systematic adjusted treatment-by-binary-biomarker interaction screen will find significant modifiers ONLY for treatment_palbociclib (with er_positive, pr_positive, her2_positive, her2_low, pik3ca_mutation), and none for the other treatments.

analyses.code: OLS pfs ~ covariates + T + B + T*B for all 6 treatments × 11 binary biomarkers
analyses.result_summary: Significant adjusted T×B interactions (p<0.001): palbociclib×er_positive (+1.559, p≈0), palbociclib×her2_positive (-1.338, p≈0), palbociclib×pik3ca_mutation (-1.665, p≈0), palbociclib×pr_positive (+1.030, p≈0), palbociclib×her2_low (+0.383, p=4e-67), palbociclib×brca2_mutation (-0.159, p=0.020). Sacituzumab×has_brain_mets (+0.195, p=0.0019). Pembrolizumab×er_positive (-0.075, p=0.028) — weak. No other treatment had any biomarker interaction with |coef|>0.1 and p<0.01. Confirms palbociclib has all the biomarker heterogeneity in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: An adjusted treatment-by-continuous-biomarker interaction screen will identify ki67_pct as a (negative) modifier of the palbociclib effect and find no comparable modifier for the other treatments.

analyses.code: OLS pfs ~ covariates + T + B + T*B for 6 treatments × 10 continuous predictors (age, ki67, tumor_size, albumin, ldh, weight_loss, crp, nlr, hemoglobin, calcium)
analyses.result_summary: Only one interaction reached p<0.01: palbociclib×ki67_pct (coef=-0.0532, p≈0). No continuous-biomarker interaction reached p<0.01 for tamoxifen, trastuzumab, olaparib, sacituzumab, or pembrolizumab.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-WT, her2_low does NOT further modify the treatment_palbociclib effect (the apparent palbociclib×her2_low interaction in the marginal screen is fully explained by the er_positive correlation with her2_low).
Within ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-WT, postmenopausal status does not meaningfully modify the treatment_palbociclib effect.

analyses.code: stratified t-test in ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-WT by her2_low
analyses.result_summary: HER2-low=1: palb +2.915 mo (n=3019 vs 5499). HER2-low=0: palb +2.903 mo (n=3552 vs 6611). Identical effect — her2_low does not add further modification once the rest of the subgroup is fixed.

analyses.code: stratified t-test in ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-WT by postmenopausal
analyses.result_summary: Postmenopausal: palb +2.819 mo (p≈0). Pre-menopausal: palb +3.042 mo (p≈0). Roughly equivalent — menopausal status does not bind.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan provides a small PFS benefit in patients with has_brain_mets=1 that is absent or reversed in those without brain mets.

analyses.code: stratified t-test by has_brain_mets; adjusted OLS interaction
analyses.result_summary: With brain mets: sacituzumab on=3.896 vs off=3.734, diff +0.162, p=0.18 (NS, n=490 vs 4600). No brain mets: -0.030 (p=0.44). Adjusted T×B interaction coef=+0.195 (p=0.0019). Direction matches the hypothesis but the within-subgroup point estimate is small and not statistically significant; treat as exploratory only.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_pembrolizumab is associated with shorter pfs_months specifically in er_positive=1 patients (negative interaction pembrolizumab×er_positive).

analyses.code: stratified t-test by er_positive; adjusted OLS interaction
analyses.result_summary: ER+: pembrolizumab on=4.77 vs off=4.88, diff -0.111, p=0.05. ER-: +0.020, p=0.50. Adjusted T×B interaction = -0.075 (p=0.028). Magnitude is tiny (~0.1 mo); likely confounding rather than a real biological signal. No clinically meaningful pembrolizumab subgroup identified.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Even unadjusted, treatment_olaparib's apparent +0.35-mo effect in BRCA1/2+ patients is not robust enough to support a real subgroup-specific benefit (p ~0.04 uncorrected, lost on adjustment).

analyses.code: adjusted OLS in BRCA1/2+ subgroup; per-mutation stratified t-tests
analyses.result_summary: Unadjusted: BRCA1+ olaparib +0.398 (p=0.12), BRCA2+ +0.295 (p=0.19), combined +0.346 (p=0.04). Adjusted (in BRCA+ subgroup, controlling for age/ecog/stage/brain/er/her2): coef=+0.044, p=0.62. Effect is not robust; should not be reported as a confirmed treatment-effect subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL: treatment_palbociclib increases pfs_months by ~+4.9 months specifically in patients with er_positive=1 AND her2_positive=0 AND pik3ca_mutation=0 AND ki67_pct<14. Outside this subgroup the palbociclib effect is small to null. PIK3CA mutation and high ki67_pct are the principal suppressors of the treatment effect within otherwise-eligible (ER+/HER2-) patients.

FINAL: treatment_tamoxifen, treatment_trastuzumab, treatment_olaparib, treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan, and treatment_pembrolizumab show no biomarker-defined subgroup with a clinically meaningful (≥0.2 mo) and adjustment-robust PFS benefit in this cohort.

analyses.code: final consolidated subgroup test in ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-WT/ki67<14, both unadjusted t-test and OLS adjusted for covariates
analyses.result_summary: Unadjusted: palbociclib on mean PFS 9.228 vs off 4.284, diff +4.944 mo, p≈0 (n=3829 on vs 6923 off). Adjusted OLS coefficient on palbociclib (controlling for age, ecog_ps, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, weight_loss_pct_6mo, albumin_g_dl, ki67_pct) = +4.977 mo, p≈0. All four predicates verified as binding via leave-one-out.

analyses.code: adjusted OLS in each canonical subgroup; full T×B interaction screens
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted effects: tamoxifen in ER+ +0.008 (p=0.67); trastuzumab in HER2+ +0.006 (p=0.60); olaparib in BRCA1/2+ +0.044 (p=0.62); sacituzumab in TNBC +0.014 (p=0.32); pembrolizumab in TNBC +0.011 (p=0.35). No subgroup search revealed any cell with a robust positive treatment effect for these five drugs.
